item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 year later but oh well.

I think that having a good researcher being clear about things that can be dangerous really makes someone look like a proper SCP agent/researcher, who are supposed to be qualified AND suited for their world-saving job.

You may be snarky and sarcastic and a bit of an asshole, but you are still gonna warn people and make them respect the protocol for their own sake.
It's really a thing I like to see in written researcher/doctors/agents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god this is one of my favorites

item.title: So, is the firearm able to bypass armor?
item.content:encoded: If I wore a vest and helmet, would I be safe?

item.title: About Translating
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm a member of SCP-JP.
and I'm going to translate this to Japanese.
So, I have a question.
I don't know What "CDC" means.
I'm waiting your response.

Sorry, I solved this myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was missing the

« SCP-2007 | SCP-2008 | SCP-2009 »

I added it, I hope that is okay.

item.title: Former or deceased U.S. presidents?
item.content:encoded: Does the first part of containment procedures only apply to wives of deceased U.S. presidents or was there an instance of a living former President appearing as a ghost?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This actually reminds me of when he was almost Assassinated, which gives me an idea
Is there a possibility for his gun to jam?
(Also +1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny enough, the slash fic mention was actually part of why I enjoyed this SCP. Given the circumstances, it felt like a shot at awkward humor to alleviate from the reality of her grim situation. It was also nice to see something absurd enough to relieve the story's ongoing tension, even if only for a moment in a throw-away line. It's short, notable, and characterizes the writer with a bit of personality (snarky and sarcastic, which fits the rest of her writing style) and a dash of healthy humanization. If anything, I'd question her mention of only continuing to read if we had proper clearance. It makes sense in the context of setting up the twist, but it felt out of place for her open and laid-back personality up to this point, and I wasn't sure why this would be a concern moments before her death when staff would automatically restrict her note to those with higher clearance. It was far from enough to ruin her dialogue for me, but it was notable enough to take me out of the audio to question why she was bringing it up.

As for containment procedures, I thought it did what it needed. No point in dragging it out, especially if containment is that simplistic. Just because it's short doesn't mean it isn't difficult, it's just that that's all you can really do to handle the entity. That's why it's Keter, after all. Overall, it was a fun read, and in my own state no less! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Sorry, Mrs. Jackson
(I am for real)
Never meant to make your husband mad
Now I'm shot and bleeding bad."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that the guy who shot back at him lived. Apparently Old Hickory isn't as tough as he thinks he is.

Will point out that for all of Jackson's flaws, one thing you can respect about him is that he followed the rules of dueling- he missed, other guy got a good hit, point was still made.

side note that I totally get Jackson's grudge both IRL and in-universe: Rachel Jackson was by all accounts a very nice lady with a sensitive personality, longstanding health issues, and emotional scars from her first marriage. There's muckraking and then there's just plain crossing the line. >: /

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure. I left the fact of her survival (at least from the duel anyway) up to the reader, but she's probably gonna die of the cancer regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that the guy who shot back at him lived. Apparently Old Hickory isn't as tough as he thinks he is.

I'll note that I can think of a couple of things for containment that might work better here, but that's not the point.

Edit: How do you feel about Strosser as an Omega-Zero saint?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is at least part of my reaction, the rest being that I just didn't get what was going on until reading Toa_Vine's post above. Like, seeing a Keter object with one sentence of containment procedures is just… How can that even stand? I guess the phenomenon is really easy to pass along to people, but you've really got to read between the lines on this, and the end result isn't quite worth the effort needed to suss it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hell yeah! This reminds me a lot of Bloody Bloody Andrew Jackson, probably because of the subject matter. Jackson as an angry spooky ghost is just a great, fun concept.
I also like that you play it seriously with the note at the end, it's an unexpected direction and surprised me quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's SCP-2006.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now the question is if this is a new scp or if the old 2008 just found found out this is what people are scared of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpick: I believe that under the more common set of standards, times afternoon but before 1 are PM, rather than AM. (But whether noon/midnight are AM or PM are fucky, but I'm under the belief that the times immediately following them have a more defined convention.)

But that's a nitpick, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While this concept is fine, this got lost on me during the last section

Mainly the line

Be glad I didn't put my 682/173 slashfic in here.

Is what drove me to downvote this. Something as out of place as this in a otherwise serious skip feels, exactly that, out of place. I get that it was meant to be something that was never found but something like this just ruins the mood of this slip. If you put something like "Be glad I didn't list off the borderline illegal things I did in high school" it may be enough for me to upvote this otherwise neat piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a good concept, and everything up until the final note is pretty intriguing. I just can't get behind that note. It makes sense but at the same time it comes and goes super quickly and suddenly, and the way information is laid out in it makes me unable to really get any kind of emotional reaction out of it.

That said, also nice job on getting voice acting for it. I always appreciate audio stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: /me waggles his eyebrows

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait a minute, is this…

+ show block
– hide block

The Ghost of Andrew Jackson shooting people who shit talk his wife, like he did when he was alive?

Cause that's awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hokay. Since I can't actually participate in the History based 4k contest, this is what my entry would've been. Would it have won? Nah probably not. But I like it anyway. Special thanks to my co-author The Great Hippo and voice actor Aaltje Luchtmeijer for her work on the project.
